_description: Synthetic QA corpus for manual skill testing. The checked-in source of truth for the fixture collection; build_collection.py turns it into a disposable run/working.anki2. Content is original and factual (facts aren't copyrightable). Decks are deliberately broad (topic-per-deck, finer subject as tags) so the skill is exercised on reusing decks + adopting the existing tag vocabulary rather than spawning sub-decks. A few notes are 'dedup targets' — facts a test prompt may re-cover, so the skill's pre-create search and neighbor net have something to catch. Grow this corpus freely; just keep it valid JSON.
_dedup_targets: Biology: mitochondria produce ATP; DNA full name; photosynthesis inputs/outputs
Geography: capital of France (Paris); longest river (Nile); highest mountain (Everest)
History: WWII end year (1945); first Roman emperor (Augustus)

fields.Front: What is the primary function of the mitochondria?
fields.Back: To produce ATP, the cell's main energy currency, via aerobic respiration.

fields.Front: Across which mitochondrial structure is the proton gradient for ATP synthesis maintained?

fields.Text: In eukaryotic cells, the citric acid cycle takes place in the {{c1::mitochondrial matrix}}.

fields.Front: What does the abbreviation DNA stand for?
Which four nitrogenous bases occur in DNA?

fields.Text: In DNA base pairing, adenine pairs with {{c1::thymine}}, and guanine pairs with {{c2::cytosine}}.

fields.Front: What is a codon?
fields.Back: A sequence of three nucleotides that specifies a single amino acid during translation.

fields.Front: What are the reactant inputs of photosynthesis?
fields.Back: Carbon dioxide and water (using light energy).

fields.Front: What are the products of photosynthesis?
Which organelle carries out photosynthesis in plant cells?
What is the main structural difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
fields.Back: Eukaryotic cells have a membrane-bound nucleus; prokaryotic cells do not.

fields.Text: The {{c1::ribosome}} is the cellular structure responsible for synthesizing proteins from messenger RNA.

fields.Front: What process do cells use to divide into two identical daughter cells?
How does meiosis differ from mitosis in its outcome?
fields.Back: Meiosis produces four genetically varied haploid cells; mitosis produces two identical diploid cells.

fields.Front: What is natural selection?
fields.Back: The process by which heritable traits that improve survival and reproduction become more common in a population over generations.

fields.Front: Whose 1859 book introduced the theory of evolution by natural selection?
fields.Back: Charles Darwin, in On the Origin of Species.

fields.Front: What is a trophic level?
fields.Back: A position in a food chain defined by how an organism obtains energy (e.g., producer, primary consumer).

fields.Text: Organisms that produce their own food from inorganic sources, such as plants, are called {{c1::autotrophs}}.

fields.Front: What gas do humans exhale as a waste product of cellular respiration?
What is the function of the cell membrane?
fields.Back: To regulate what enters and leaves the cell, maintaining selective permeability.

fields.Front: In what year did World War II end?
What event on 1 September 1939 is generally taken as the start of World War II in Europe?
What was the code name for the Allied invasion of Normandy in June 1944?
Who was the first Roman emperor?
In what year was Julius Caesar assassinated?

fields.Text: The Western Roman Empire is traditionally said to have fallen in the year {{c1::476}} AD.

fields.Front: In which country did the Renaissance begin?
Which Florentine artist painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel?
In what year did the French Revolution begin?
What Parisian fortress was stormed on 14 July 1789?
In what year did the United States declare independence?

fields.Text: The American Declaration of Independence was primarily drafted by {{c1::Thomas Jefferson}}.

fields.Front: What invention by Johannes Gutenberg, around 1440, transformed the spread of information?
Which empire was ruled by Genghis Khan?
What was the Roman Senate's role under the Republic?
fields.Back: It was the chief deliberative and advisory body, controlling finances and foreign policy.

fields.Front: la manzana
el pan

comer

What is the Spanish first-person singular present of 'ser' (to be)?
When do Spanish speakers use 'ser' rather than 'estar' for 'to be'?
fields.Back: For permanent or essential characteristics (identity, origin, time), rather than temporary states or location.

fields.Front: What is the capital of France?
What is the capital of Japan?
What is the capital of Australia?
What is the capital of Canada?
What is the capital of Brazil?
Which is the longest river in the world?
fields.Back: The Nile (by the traditional measure; the Amazon is the largest by volume).

fields.Front: On which river does the city of London sit?

fields.Text: The {{c1::Amazon}} River carries more water than any other river on Earth.

fields.Front: What is the highest mountain above sea level on Earth?
Which mountain range contains Mount Everest?
What is the largest ocean on Earth?

fields.Text: The {{c1::Atlantic}} Ocean separates the Americas from Europe and Africa.

fields.Front: Which line of latitude divides Earth into Northern and Southern hemispheres?
How many continents are there by the common seven-continent model?
What is the capital of Egypt?
